description: Curated public work log entries for sainsbury.ai. Keep entries public-safe and high level; do not include private task state, raw clinical details, inbox content, or collaborator-sensitive material.

items.title: Public work-log workflow and site updates
items.summary: Refined the public site workflow around sainsbury.ai, including a curated work-log page and clearer outputs/project presentation.
items: Published and browser-tested a curated public work-log backfill for recent website and research-infrastructure updates.
Tightened public projects/output headings and expanded the software/infrastructure outputs panel.
Improved the guarded local path from internal Cog logs to public-safe work-log candidates.

items.title: Public website launch and clinical-sequence modelling tooling
items.summary: Launched the first public version of sainsbury.ai and made a concentrated pass across clinical-sequence modelling infrastructure, public research profile plumbing, and the Cog/Nightshift work-log system.
items: Built and deployed the first sainsbury.ai static site on GitHub Pages, connected the custom domain, enabled HTTPS, and added the initial Projects, Outputs, and Work log pages.
Seeded the Outputs page from the public Google Scholar profile and added a review-only monthly check for publication updates.

Advanced open clinical-sequence modelling tooling with several synthetic-test scaffolds and evaluation harness improvements for horizon specification, autoregressive latent prediction, and future-summary readouts.
Captured a new modelling direction around probabilistic EHR belief states: treating records as partial observations of an evolving latent clinical state rather than as fixed snapshots.
Added an internal cross-project Cog work-log generator so daily progress can be reviewed chronologically while keeping public updates curated and safe.

items.title: Clinical-JEPA evaluation gates and remote-compute supervision
items.summary: Focused on clinical-sequence modelling infrastructure: prior-art framing, autoregression gates, target-horizon checks, and safer remote-compute lifecycle tooling.
items: Ingested and framed Clinical-JEPA prior art and related control-grid ideas.
Built and reviewed successive autoregression and horizon-specification gates for clinical-sequence modelling work.
Designed and smoke-tested a remote GPU lifecycle supervisor for safer launch/sync/destroy workflows.
Reviewed the modelling path forward and shifted toward a clearer target-window/horizon redesign.

items.title: Scout infrastructure and literature retrieval bridges
items.summary: Expanded Cog's scout-wave infrastructure with literature-retrieval bridges, route-memory reporting, and status/blueprint workflow surfaces.
items: Added and iterated OpenAlex search/hydration bridges for scout waves and broader literature recall.
Connected glucose.ai literature radar into Cog's domain-specific research-query workflow.
Developed blueprint/status workflow surfaces for tracking autonomous or long-running research threads.
Ran JEPA scout-wave retests to diagnose and improve literature retrieval behaviour.

items.title: Literature radar and Clinical-JEPA framing
items.summary: Built out literature-radar memory around curated papers and used it to sharpen a Clinical-JEPA disease-fingerprinting research direction.
items: Added a Paperpile literature-radar bridge so curated papers can better inform project planning and recall.
Synthesised latent disease fingerprinting with Clinical-JEPA as a clearer research thread.
Ingested a JEPA/autoregression paper batch into the maintained knowledge base for later modelling and writing work.

items.title: Cog architecture cleanup and open-source workflow watchlist
items.summary: Refined Cog's project memory and reviewed external/open-source agent workflow ideas that might be useful for local research infrastructure.
items: Updated the open-source project watchlist and sweep notes for agent/workflow systems worth monitoring.
Cleaned Cog architecture/state surfaces so sources, tasks, synthesis, and workflow memory remain easier to navigate.
Kept sensitive planning material out of the public work-log surface while preserving internal continuity in Cog.
